canonical_definition: Across the provided sources, K-means clustering is described as a statistical procedure used to group data points based on similarity. The prevailing application is to examine whether items form distinct groups in a high-dimensional space. For example, one paper uses K-means clustering on "LLM preference vectors" to group languages and identify "clusters of behavioral patterns." A different application involves using the method on "prompt embeddings" to see if they form natural groupings. This work mentions employing K-means clustering as one of two analytical methods, alongside "quantifying token importance." The procedure is implemented by running the algorithm with a pre-specified number of centers.
papers: Differentiation and Specialization of Attention Heads via the Refined Local Learning Coefficient
Language Model Alignment in Multilingual Trolley Problems
Rapid Selection and Ordering of In-Context Demonstrations via Prompt Embedding Clustering

definitions.text: A statistical analysis method used to group languages based on the similarity of LLM preference vectors, identifying clusters of behavioral patterns.
definitions.paper: Language Model Alignment in Multilingual Trolley Problems

definitions.text: A clustering procedure used to examine whether prompt embeddings form groups in high-dimensional space.
definitions.paper: Rapid Selection and Ordering of In-Context Demonstrations via Prompt Embedding Clustering
